pattern.use_when: Need to inspect collaborators, send invitations, update permissions, or remove members from workspaces, rooms, or murals.

sequences.sequence: getroommembers → inviteuserstoroom → updateroommemberpermissions (body: userId + permission) → removeroomusers (body: userIds[])
getmuralusers → inviteuserstomural → updatemuralmemberpermissions (PATH: userId; body: permission only) → removemuralusers (body: userIds[])
inviteuserstoworkspace (requires workspaces:write; caller must be workspace admin) → getroommembers or getmuralusers to verify provisioning downstream

pattern.critical_asymmetry: updatemuralmemberpermissions and updateroommemberpermissions are NOT symmetric. - Mural variant: PATCH /murals/{muralId}/users/{userId}/permissions — userId is in the path; only permission in the body. - Room variant: POST /rooms/{roomId}/users/permissions — userId and permission are both in the body. Confusing these shapes is a common implementation defect.
pattern.scope_notes: - rooms:write covers room invitation, permission update, and removal. - murals:write covers mural invitation, permission update, and removal. - workspaces:write covers workspace invitation only; no equivalent remove or update-permission endpoint exists at workspace level in the public API. - users:read is needed alongside write scopes for pre-flight membership inspection.
